ip_outcome_note: Card name is 'Hirudegarn, the Wanderer.' The bipedal demon form depicted — upright, large, with a demonic face — matches Hirudegarn's second evolved form after metamorphosis. The wanderer epithet matches lore: Hirudegarn has no mind or purpose beyond destruction, drifting and destroying.

trivia_bullets.text: The 'Wanderer' epithet reflects Hirudegarn's canonical characterization: the creature has no intelligence, will, or mission — it is described as having 'no sense of reason' and simply destroying everything in its path [WebSearch: Tapion Minotia Konatsian lore, Dragon Ball Fandom: Hirudegarn]. The humanoid demon form depicted on this card is Hirudegarn's second form, reached via metamorphosis — distinguished from the insectoid first form on BT4-037 by its upright bipedal stance and visible demonic face.

Dragon Ball Z: Wrath of the Dragon (1995) was the thirteenth Dragon Ball Z theatrical film, released in Japan on July 15, 1995. It was the final DBZ film produced during the original Z run [Wikipedia: Dragon Ball Z: Wrath of the Dragon]. Hirudegarn's final defeat comes not from a conventional energy attack but from Goku's Dragon Fist technique — a move that punches through Hirudegarn's body — marking one of the Dragon Fist's first prominent appearances in the franchise.
sources: Wikipedia: Dragon Ball Z: Wrath of the Dragon

trivia_bullets.text: BT4-038 is a Common rarity card (card no. 038/124 in Colossal Warfare), one of the lower-rarity Hirudegarn representations in a set that included dedicated Hirudegarn combo mechanics. The set's design concept noted that the lower half of Hirudegarn's sealed form sets up conditions for playing the Impenetrable Defense version (BT4-037), establishing a multi-card progression arc across commons and uncommons [WebSearch: DBS BT4 Colossal Warfare design overview].
sources: WebSearch: Colossal Warfare checklist Bleeding Cool
